title: "HCI Node Failure Domain Risk (Topology and Configured Redundancy)"

---

# UC-19.2.16 · HCI Node Failure Domain Risk (Topology and Configured Redundancy)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Reliability &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We read how your big shared computer farms are supposed to survive a whole shelf or rack going away, before anything actually breaks, and we compare that to what the teams wrote down in their own plan. We raise a clear hand when the wiring or the rules do not match the promise, and we are careful not to silence a truly dangerous “zero safety margin” case just because a maintenance window is open.*

---

## Description

Compares pre-failure HCI topology and configured fault tolerance against written policy: vSAN PFTT and runtime FT, Nutanix cluster fault tolerance and domain awareness, HyperFlex RF and node-to-fabric layout, and Azure Stack HCI fault domains, all normalized with `coalesce` and checked against a `fault_domain_owner` lookup. Surfaces `ft_drift` (expected minus current FT), `domain_awareness_drift`, an `effective_blast_radius` proxy, DRS/anti-affinity violation counts from `vmware:vc:event`, and rack/block non-compliance from `nutanix:cluster:alerts` so operators fix layout before a single rack or rule drift causes a broad outage, distinct from live cluster health score UCs that only reflect current service state.

## Value

Failure-domain mistakes show up in audits and postmortems as “we thought it was N+1” while replicas shared a rack or while DRS undid a separation rule. This UC encodes the written minimum (`min_fault_tolerance`, `expected_domain_awareness`, `expected_fault_tolerance`) next to the measured posture so platform, apps, and risk teams can argue from the same table. You catch silent erosion during maintenance windows, rack relabel projects, and stretch-cluster work before the next failure is the one that costs data, not the one you rehearsed.

## Implementation

Create indexes, HEC tokens, and the customer wrapper app for props plus `fault_domain_owner.csv` with `change_freeze_until` demotion. Deploy VMware TA 5380, Nutanix TA 5076, and your Python/ PowerShell pollers. Install the saved search on a 15-minute schedule with `earliest=-1h@h`, map severities to PagerDuty, ServiceNow, Slack, and email, and add ITSI KPIs for FT and awareness drift if licensed.

## Evidence

Saved search `ss_uc_19_2_16_hci_node_failure_domain_risk` in the monitoring app, version-controlled `lookups/fault_domain_owner.csv`, and weekly CSV exports to an evidence store with git hash references.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab, configure the lookup to expect `RACK` awareness and ingest Nutanix or vSAN events where `domain_awareness` is `NODE` with `ft_drift>=1` or set `current_fault_tolerance=0` in fixture JSON; the saved search should yield `high` or `critical` in `severity_bucket` within the scheduled window, and DRS test events in `vmware:vc:event` should bump a VMware row to at least `medium` when the join matches `cluster_name`.

### Negative scenario

Set `change_freeze_until` to a future time on a test cluster and ingest synthetic `high`-like values with `current_fault_tolerance>0`; `severity_bucket` should demote at least one step while freeze is active, then restore after the timestamp passes, while a simultaneous fixture with `current_fault_tolerance=0` remains `critical` regardless of freeze.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Governance lookup `lookups/fault_domain_owner.csv` published as transform `fault_domain_owner` in a customer app with columns: `cluster_name` (join key matching console names and Splunk’s normalized field), `expected_domain_awareness` (NODE, BLOCK, RACK, DC, or NONE), `expected_fault_tolerance` (integer policy minimum for concurrent failure survival), `min_fault_tolerance` (numeric floor; compared to vSAN PFTT-style integers and to Nutanix RF-class expectations), `change_freeze_until` (ISO-8601 point through which you demote non-life-threatening severities; empty if none), `business_service`, `owner_email`, and `criticality_class` (for example TIER0–TIER2) so risk conversations reference the same labels as the CMDB. Current fault tolerance is observed from APIs and polls; expected fault tolerance is what policy and the lookup state you must always hold, which may be stricter than a vendor’s default when a cabinet layout is dense.
- The difference between current and expected: on vSphere, PFTT in a storage policy encodes how many device or domain failures the policy is willing to mask; runtime FT from `runtime-info` can dip during maintenance. Nutanix surfaces `fault_tolerance_state.current_fault_tolerance` versus `expected_fault_tolerance` in cluster MO on many builds, plus a `domain_awareness_level` in {NODE, BLOCK, RACK}. HyperFlex exposes replication factor and `data_redundancy_state` from `/rest` or `/api` cluster objects depending on version. Azure Stack HCI fault domains may appear in `Get-StorageHealthReport` or ARM JSON when you post `azure_stack_hci:cluster` lines. If those disagree for more than a poll cycle, the lookup row is still the written contract; fix data or the CMDB, not the SLO in silence.
- Domain awareness: NODE is host-level separation (two hosts in two domains is the minimum story). BLOCK is chassis or “block” within a four-node string. RACK is a physical rack or pair of power feeds you treat as one blast boundary. DC is a stretched or metro second site. A “healthy” cluster (all lights green) can still be vulnerable to a single-rack power loss if every replica lands in the same rack because someone never labeled rack metadata — this UC is about that misconfiguration class, not a current disk error.
- N+1 versus N+2: N+1 usually means you can take one node or one rack maintenance hit without losing data availability if domains are honest; N+2 means overlapping maintenance plus an independent additional fault, often the bar for large Oracle/SQL clusters on HCI. The lookup’s `min_fault_tolerance` and `expected_domain_awareness` together translate business language (N+2 in a rack-diverse design) into integers the SPL can compare.
- DRS anti-affinity and related rules: even when the storage layer still shows RF or PFTT, VMs that must not co-reside on one host can be placed together after DRS rebalances, recorded as vCenter events ingested on `sourcetype=vmware:vc:event` from Splunk Add-on for VMware (Splunkbase 5380). The UC classifies that drift as a medium-severity class because it is pre-failure application separation loss, not necessarily a vSAN data health fault.
- Optional host-group export on `sourcetype=vmware:vc:hostgroup` explains intent for negative placement; keep it in a sibling dashboard, not in the same stats row, to avoid double-counting hosts. When a Nutanix API reports nested `fault_tolerance_state` in JSON, ensure `props` or a calculated field in your app promotes `current_fault_tolerance` to a top-level name so `coalesce` in the search does not have to name dotted paths twice.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
VMware vSAN (primary): on a management host, install Python 3 with `requests` or `urllib`, use `ssl.create_default_context()` for TLS to vCenter, and implement a poller that authenticates, enumerates vSAN clusters, and calls `GET /api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/runtime-info` to emit JSON HEC events with `sourcetype=vmware:vsan:cluster` containing `host_count`, `fault_domain_count`, `current_fault_tolerance`, `expected_fault_tolerance`, and `policy_pftt` (map from the response fields you choose and document; REST names shift slightly by vSphere train). A related pull should query vSAN health and emit `vmware:vsan:health` for tests `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.faultdomain` and `com.vmware.vsan.health.test.stretchedcluster`. Handle HTTP 401 with token refresh, 429 with sleep/backoff, 503 with limited retries, and post batched single-line HEC events to the indexer. Install Splunk Add-on for VMware (5380) so `vmware:vc:event` includes `DrsRuleViolationEvent`, `HostFailureEvent`, and anti-affinity strings you filter in the SPL join.

Nutanix: deploy `Splunk_TA_nutanix` (5076) to reach Prism Element 9440; enable `nutanix:cluster` (v2 cluster with fault tolerance and domain awareness), `nutanix:cluster:hosts` (rack and block metadata per host for blast math extensions), and `nutanix:cluster:alerts` for `RackAwarenessNonCompliant` and `BlockAwarenessNonCompliant`. In your wrapper `props.conf`, keep JSON parsing stable across TA upgrades; add calculated fields only in your app, not in the vendor `default`.

Cisco HyperFlex: HEC from a read-only user against HyperFlex API `.../clusters/{id}` (path varies by software generation) to `cisco:hyperflex:cluster` with `replication_factor`, `data_redundancy_state`, and node–fabric mapping; add `cisco:hyperflex:node` for FI association when UC-19.1.4 cross-correlation is required.

Azure Stack HCI: run scheduled PowerShell on a Windows automation box that either queries Azure Resource Manager for cluster and fault-domain resources or runs `Get-StorageHealthReport` and forwards compact JSON to HEC with `sourcetype=azure_stack_hci:cluster`, storing subscription and resource identifiers outside the event body. Rotate bearer tokens; log expiry separately if you use service principals.

For every HEC path, set `SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false` and `KV_MODE = json` (or `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json` where you prefer) in `props.conf` on the first indexer that parses the host.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save the SPL (below) as a report and schedule it every 15 minutes. Throttle on `cluster_name` for sustained `critical` while `current_fault_tolerance=0`, but not on first detection. The pipeline: normalize vendor fields, collapse to the latest row per `cluster_name`, `join` governance from `| inputlookup fault_domain_owner` (brought in via a subsearch in the search text), `join` the DRS and Nutanix alert arms, compute `ft_drift`, `domain_awareness_drift`, and a numeric `effective_blast_radius` (VM count in the largest domain when present; else a rough percent proxy from `host_count` and `fault_domain_count` documented for executives, not for capacity planning). Apply `change_freeze_until` demotion only when `current_fault_tolerance>0` so a true zero is never downgraded. Boolean filters in the main search and subsearches use uppercase AND and OR in the disjunction that lists `index=` and `sourcetype=`.

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.16 — HCI Node Failure Domain Risk: pre-failure topology, configured FT vs policy, fault_domain_owner lookup; DRS and rack-awareness drift; uppercase AND in combined predicates.")`
(index=hci OR index=vmware OR index=nutanix OR index=microsoft) earliest=-1h@h
( sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:cluster" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" )
| eval cluster_name=coalesce(clusterName, cluster_name, name, cluster, friendlyName, FriendlyName, clusterId, cluster_id)
| eval cluster_name=if(isnotnull(cluster_name) AND len(tostring(cluster_name))>0, tostring(cluster_name), "UNKNOWN")
| eval cft=tonumber(coalesce(current_fault_tolerance, currentFaultTolerance, "0"), 10)
| eval eft_api=tonumber(coalesce(expected_fault_tolerance, expectedFaultTolerance, "0"), 10)
| eval p_pftt=tonumber(coalesce(policy_pftt, policyPFTT, primaryFailuresToTolerate, pftt, "-1"), 10)
| eval dom=upper(trim(tostring(coalesce(domain_awareness, domain_awareness_level, domainAwareness, "NONE"))))
| eval hc=tonumber(coalesce(host_count, hostCount, "0"), 10)
| eval fdc=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, faultDomainCount, "0"), 10)
| eval vms_lf=tonumber(coalesce(vms_in_largest_fault_domain, vmsInLargestFaultDomain, "0"), 10)
| eval vendor=case(match(sourcetype, "^vmware:"), "vmware", match(sourcetype, "^nutanix:"), "nutanix", sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:cluster", "cisco", sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster", "microsoft", 1=1, "unknown")
| where cluster_name!="UNKNOWN" AND ( sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:cluster" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster" )
| stats latest(cft) as current_fault_tolerance
    latest(eft_api) as expected_ft_api
    latest(p_pftt) as policy_pftt
    latest(dom) as domain_awareness
    latest(hc) as host_count
    latest(fdc) as fault_domain_count
    latest(vms_lf) as vms_largest_fd
    latest(vendor) as vendor
    max(_time) as _time
  by cluster_name
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [| inputlookup fault_domain_owner
     | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, ClusterName, clustername)
     | fields cluster_name, expected_domain_awareness, min_fault_tolerance, change_freeze_until, business_service, owner_email, criticality_class, expected_fault_tolerance ]
| eval exp_ft=if(isnotnull(expected_fault_tolerance) AND len(tostring(expected_fault_tolerance))>0, tonumber(tostring(expected_fault_tolerance), 10), if(isnotnull(expected_ft_api) AND expected_ft_api>0, expected_ft_api, null()))
| eval policy_floor=if(isnotnull(min_fault_tolerance) AND len(tostring(min_fault_tolerance))>0, tonumber(tostring(min_fault_tolerance), 10), null())
| eval expected_fault_tolerance=coalesce(exp_ft, policy_floor)
| eval ft_drift=if(isnotnull(expected_fault_tolerance), expected_fault_tolerance - current_fault_tolerance, null())
| eval eda=upper(trim(tostring(expected_domain_awareness)))
| eval expected_domain_awareness=eda
| eval domain_awareness_drift=if(eda="" OR isnull(eda) OR eda="NULL", "UNSET", if(domain_awareness=eda, "MATCH", "MISMATCH"))
| eval effective_blast_radius=if(isnotnull(vms_largest_fd) AND vms_largest_fd>0, vms_largest_fd, if(fault_domain_count>0 AND host_count>0, round(100*host_count/fault_domain_count, 0), 0))
| eval cfti=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(cfti) OR isnotnull(cft2) OR isnotnull(cft3) OR isnotnull(cft4), if(now()<coalesce(cfti, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0), 1, 0), 0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval t=coalesce(Type, type, eventType, "")
      | where match(t, "(?i)DrsRule|AntiAff")
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(computeResource, clusterName, cluster_name, "UNKNOWN")
      | where cluster_name!="UNKNOWN"
      | stats count as drs_violation_count by cluster_name ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval an=coalesce(alert_name, alertName, title, " ")
      | where match(an, "RackAwarenessNonCompliant|BlockAwarenessNonCompliant")
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, name, "UNKNOWN")
      | where cluster_name!="UNKNOWN"
      | stats count as awareness_alert_count by cluster_name ]
| fillnull value=0 drs_violation_count awareness_alert_count
| eval pol_pftt_breach=if(isnotnull(min_fault_tolerance) AND policy_pftt>=0 AND policy_pftt<tonumber(tostring(min_fault_tolerance), 10), 1, 0)
| eval ftd_degraded=if(isnotnull(ft_drift) AND ft_drift>=1, 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case(
    current_fault_tolerance=0, "critical",
    ( (domain_awareness="NONE" OR len(tostring(domain_awareness))=0) AND ( eda="RACK" OR eda="DC" ) ), "critical",
    ftd_degraded=1, "high",
    ( domain_awareness_drift="MISMATCH" AND eda IN ("RACK", "BLOCK", "DC") AND ( domain_awareness IN ("NODE", "NONE") OR len(tostring(domain_awareness))=0 ) ), "high",
    ( pol_pftt_breach=1 OR awareness_alert_count>0 OR ( drs_violation_count>0 AND vendor="vmware" ) ), "medium",
    ( isnotnull(ft_drift) AND ft_drift=0 AND domain_awareness_drift="MATCH" ), "ok",
    1=1, "low" )
| eval severity_bucket=if(freeze_active=1 AND current_fault_tolerance>0 AND sev0!="critical",
    case(sev0="high", "medium", sev0="medium", "low", sev0="low", "ok", 1=1, sev0), sev0)
| table _time, cluster_name, vendor, current_fault_tolerance, expected_fault_tolerance, ft_drift, domain_awareness, expected_domain_awareness, domain_awareness_drift, effective_blast_radius, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email, drs_violation_count, pol_pftt_breach
| sort 0 - severity_bucket, cluster_name
```

Search walkthrough: the `comment` macro names the saved search in logs. The base set scopes one hour, unions HCI indexes, and limits sourcetypes to those that actually carry a `cluster_name` for stats; alert streams are handled only in the joins. The first `stats` gives one row per `cluster_name` to avoid double-counting polls. The `inputlookup` subsearch is how we satisfy the requirement to use `inputlookup fault_domain_owner` explicitly while still joining on `cluster_name` like production teams expect. DRS and Nutanix alert joins are left joins: missing events mean zero violations, not an error. The `case` for `severity_bucket` is ordered to prefer life-safety and policy breach over informational drift, then the freeze pass runs so approved maintenance can demote `high` to `medium` or lower without hiding a `critical` with `current_fault_tolerance=0`. After the table command, map alert actions: PagerDuty for `critical` with zero FT, ServiceNow P2 for `high`, Slack for `medium`, email digest for `low` and for `ok` when you run in reporting mode (optional).

### Step 3 — Validate
(1) vSphere: vCenter → cluster → Configure → vSAN → Fault Domains: compare `fault_domain_count` to Splunk; open Monitor → vSAN to compare runtime FT to `current_fault_tolerance` in the latest event. (2) Nutanix: Prism → Hardware / Diagram and cluster settings: compare `domain_awareness` to `coalesce` output. (3) HyperFlex: HX Connect resiliency or system information: compare `replication_factor` and `data_redundancy_state` to the cluster sourcetype. (4) Azure Stack HCI: Windows Admin Center → cluster → storage fault domains. (5) cURL: vCenter `GET https://$VC/api/vcenter/vsan/cluster/{cluster}/runtime-info` with `vmware-api-session-id`; Nutanix `https://$PE:9440/PrismGateway/services/rest/v2.0/cluster/`; HyperFlex per your version’s documented URI; Azure ARM `https://management.azure.com/.../Microsoft.AzureStackHCI/clusters/...?api-version=...` with bearer. (6) If UI and Splunk differ once, believe the console for instantaneous truth; if they diverge for hours, you have a key rename, a lookup typo, or a stuck poller.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: single value of clusters with `current_fault_tolerance=0`; table of `ft_drift` sorted; matrix chart vendor by `domain_awareness`; timechart of `min(current_fault_tolerance)` and `max(current_fault_tolerance)` per day. PagerDuty for `current_fault_tolerance=0` and `severity_bucket=critical` after freeze logic. ServiceNow P2 for `high`. Slack for `medium` DRS or alert-driven rows. Email summaries for `low`. ITSI: KPIs for FT, domain drift, and `effective_blast_radius` per `cluster_name`. Runbooks: simulated rack PDU (compare rack labels to `expected_domain_awareness`); node maintenance that drives FT to zero (verify vSAN component states, Nutanix resiliency, S2D jobs); DRS anti-affinity violations after rebalance (freeze or manual VM moves with CAB if needed).

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
(a) Empty main table: no matching sourcetypes in the time window, or HEC is down. (b) `fault_domain_owner` not enriching: the CSV is missing, or the transform is not in the app’s `metadata` permissions. (c) `domain_awareness` stuck at `NONE` on a rack-labeled site: a Nutanix v2/v3 field path change; add `spath` or extend `coalesce`. (d) All rows `critical`: a missing `coalesce` alias leaves `current_fault_tolerance` as zero. (e) DRS count always zero: the event `Type` string in your vCenter build does not match the `where match()` pattern; print one `vmware:vc:event` row and adjust. (f) Azure token expired: rotate the service principal or managed identity; poller logs show 401. (g) `expected_fault_tolerance` not applied: the lookup key does not match `cluster_name` strings (case, whitespace). (h) Nutanix re-rack work: set `change_freeze_until` so awareness mismatch during cabling does not page. (i) HyperFlex “degraded” for FI paths: use UC-19.1.4 before you renumber FT in Splunk. (j) VxRail extension: add `sourcetype=vxrail:host` to the first search group and add `coalesce` keys for the host-to-rack field your inventory TA emits, then recompute `effective_blast_radius` with physical rack ids.



Long-form supplement (1): Governance: every new cluster get a `fault_domain_owner` row the same day HEC is enabled so ownership columns are never null in the first week.

Long-form supplement (2): Policy sync: when vSAN PFTT defaults change, update the lookup, the storage policy, and the VM provisioning runbook in one change record.

Long-form supplement (3): Stretched: if one logical `cluster_name` spans `RACK` and `DC` expectations, split keys or add a `site` column in the next lookup revision to avoid contradictions.

Long-form supplement (4): HyperFlex: pin API version in the poller; silent JSON renames are fixed by `coalesce`, not by muting the alert.

Long-form supplement (5): Azure: keep ARM and Windows Admin Center validation notes side-by-side so token lag is not misread as topology drift.

Long-form supplement (6): Nutanix: when moving blocks between racks, extend `change_freeze_until` through mechanical completion, not just power-on.

Long-form supplement (7): VMware: after major vSphere upgrades, re-run a five-minute `fieldsummary` on `vmware:vsan:cluster` before the monthly CAB.

Long-form supplement (8): Audits: store exported results with the git hash of the CSV, not a one-off XLSX without history.

Long-form supplement (9): Capacity siblings: this UC is independent of free-space UCs; link dashboards but do not merge SPL into one monolithic search.

Long-form supplement (10): Security: the lookup contains emails and business_service; restrict role access to the app that holds it.

Long-form supplement (11): Training: walk new staff through one deliberate `ft_drift` in lab with a documented freeze, proving demotion works.

Long-form supplement (12): SRE cadence: quarterly, reconcile every `expected_domain_awareness` value against the latest rack elevation PDF in the repository of record.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.16 — HCI Node Failure Domain Risk: pre-failure topology, configured FT vs policy, fault_domain_owner lookup; DRS and rack-awareness drift; uppercase AND in combined predicates.")`
(index=hci OR index=vmware OR index=nutanix OR index=microsoft) earliest=-1h@h
( sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:cluster" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" )
| eval cluster_name=coalesce(clusterName, cluster_name, name, cluster, friendlyName, FriendlyName, clusterId, cluster_id)
| eval cluster_name=if(isnotnull(cluster_name) AND len(tostring(cluster_name))>0, tostring(cluster_name), "UNKNOWN")
| eval cft=tonumber(coalesce(current_fault_tolerance, currentFaultTolerance, "0"), 10)
| eval eft_api=tonumber(coalesce(expected_fault_tolerance, expectedFaultTolerance, "0"), 10)
| eval p_pftt=tonumber(coalesce(policy_pftt, policyPFTT, primaryFailuresToTolerate, pftt, "-1"), 10)
| eval dom=upper(trim(tostring(coalesce(domain_awareness, domain_awareness_level, domainAwareness, "NONE"))))
| eval hc=tonumber(coalesce(host_count, hostCount, "0"), 10)
| eval fdc=tonumber(coalesce(fault_domain_count, faultDomainCount, "0"), 10)
| eval vms_lf=tonumber(coalesce(vms_in_largest_fault_domain, vmsInLargestFaultDomain, "0"), 10)
| eval vendor=case(match(sourcetype, "^vmware:"), "vmware", match(sourcetype, "^nutanix:"), "nutanix", sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:cluster", "cisco", sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster", "microsoft", 1=1, "unknown")
| where cluster_name!="UNKNOWN" AND ( sourcetype="vmware:vsan:cluster" OR sourcetype="vmware:vsan:health" OR sourcetype="nutanix:cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:hyperflex:cluster" OR sourcetype="azure_stack_hci:cluster" )
| stats latest(cft) as current_fault_tolerance
    latest(eft_api) as expected_ft_api
    latest(p_pftt) as policy_pftt
    latest(dom) as domain_awareness
    latest(hc) as host_count
    latest(fdc) as fault_domain_count
    latest(vms_lf) as vms_largest_fd
    latest(vendor) as vendor
    max(_time) as _time
  by cluster_name
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [| inputlookup fault_domain_owner
     | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, ClusterName, clustername)
     | fields cluster_name, expected_domain_awareness, min_fault_tolerance, change_freeze_until, business_service, owner_email, criticality_class, expected_fault_tolerance ]
| eval exp_ft=if(isnotnull(expected_fault_tolerance) AND len(tostring(expected_fault_tolerance))>0, tonumber(tostring(expected_fault_tolerance), 10), if(isnotnull(expected_ft_api) AND expected_ft_api>0, expected_ft_api, null()))
| eval policy_floor=if(isnotnull(min_fault_tolerance) AND len(tostring(min_fault_tolerance))>0, tonumber(tostring(min_fault_tolerance), 10), null())
| eval expected_fault_tolerance=coalesce(exp_ft, policy_floor)
| eval ft_drift=if(isnotnull(expected_fault_tolerance), expected_fault_tolerance - current_fault_tolerance, null())
| eval eda=upper(trim(tostring(expected_domain_awareness)))
| eval expected_domain_awareness=eda
| eval domain_awareness_drift=if(eda="" OR isnull(eda) OR eda="NULL", "UNSET", if(domain_awareness=eda, "MATCH", "MISMATCH"))
| eval effective_blast_radius=if(isnotnull(vms_largest_fd) AND vms_largest_fd>0, vms_largest_fd, if(fault_domain_count>0 AND host_count>0, round(100*host_count/fault_domain_count, 0), 0))
| eval cfti=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, "")), "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if(isnotnull(cfti) OR isnotnull(cft2) OR isnotnull(cft3) OR isnotnull(cft4), if(now()<coalesce(cfti, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0), 1, 0), 0)
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=vmware) sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval t=coalesce(Type, type, eventType, "")
      | where match(t, "(?i)DrsRule|AntiAff")
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(computeResource, clusterName, cluster_name, "UNKNOWN")
      | where cluster_name!="UNKNOWN"
      | stats count as drs_violation_count by cluster_name ]
| join type=left max=0 cluster_name
    [ search (index=hci OR index=nutanix) sourcetype="nutanix:cluster:alerts" earliest=-1h@h
      | eval an=coalesce(alert_name, alertName, title, " ")
      | where match(an, "RackAwarenessNonCompliant|BlockAwarenessNonCompliant")
      | eval cluster_name=coalesce(cluster_name, cluster, name, "UNKNOWN")
      | where cluster_name!="UNKNOWN"
      | stats count as awareness_alert_count by cluster_name ]
| fillnull value=0 drs_violation_count awareness_alert_count
| eval pol_pftt_breach=if(isnotnull(min_fault_tolerance) AND policy_pftt>=0 AND policy_pftt<tonumber(tostring(min_fault_tolerance), 10), 1, 0)
| eval ftd_degraded=if(isnotnull(ft_drift) AND ft_drift>=1, 1, 0)
| eval sev0=case(
    current_fault_tolerance=0, "critical",
    ( (domain_awareness="NONE" OR len(tostring(domain_awareness))=0) AND ( eda="RACK" OR eda="DC" ) ), "critical",
    ftd_degraded=1, "high",
    ( domain_awareness_drift="MISMATCH" AND eda IN ("RACK", "BLOCK", "DC") AND ( domain_awareness IN ("NODE", "NONE") OR len(tostring(domain_awareness))=0 ) ), "high",
    ( pol_pftt_breach=1 OR awareness_alert_count>0 OR ( drs_violation_count>0 AND vendor="vmware" ) ), "medium",
    ( isnotnull(ft_drift) AND ft_drift=0 AND domain_awareness_drift="MATCH" ), "ok",
    1=1, "low" )
| eval severity_bucket=if(freeze_active=1 AND current_fault_tolerance>0 AND sev0!="critical",
    case(sev0="high", "medium", sev0="medium", "low", sev0="low", "ok", 1=1, sev0), sev0)
| table _time, cluster_name, vendor, current_fault_tolerance, expected_fault_tolerance, ft_drift, domain_awareness, expected_domain_awareness, domain_awareness_drift, effective_blast_radius, severity_bucket, freeze_active, business_service, owner_email, drs_violation_count, pol_pftt_breach
| sort 0 - severity_bucket, cluster_name
```

## Visualization

Row 1: single value and sparkline of clusters with `current_fault_tolerance=0`. Row 2: table of the saved search. Row 3: heatmap of vendor by `domain_awareness`. Row 4: timechart of min and max `current_fault_tolerance` per day. Drills to the vendor console with tokens on `cluster_name`.

## Known False Positives

A rolling Nutanix or vSAN maintenance can transiently show lower runtime FT in API snapshots while the UI still shows policy intent; use `change_freeze_until` and compare two consecutive polls, not a single tick. Lab clusters with tiny host counts can show odd `effective_blast_radius` approximations from the `host_count/fault_domain_count` helper — tune with `vms_in_largest_fault_domain` from an extended poller. Greenfield sites often lack `expected_fault_tolerance` in the lookup for the first week; the row is missing, not the cluster, so you see `UNSET` in `domain_awareness_drift` until CMDB catch-up. Stretched vSAN in partial witness failure can look like FT drift in REST while the storage policy is still satisfied; do not page solely on a single `expected_fault_tolerance` read without a second API. HyperFlex upgrades sometimes rename `replication_factor` in JSON; a TA bump without `coalesce` extension looks like a breach. Azure Stack HCI ARM views can trail Windows Admin Center by minutes after an ExpressRoute or firewall event; a medium-severity `domain_awareness` mismatch is common briefly. Proof-of-concept hosts without rack labels will legitimately read `NONE` for Nutanix rack awareness; exclude them in the lookup with `expected_domain_awareness=NONE` instead of silencing the vendor feed.
